title: Share of artificial intelligence jobs among all job postings
subtitle: A job posting is considered an AI job if it requests one or more AI skills, e.g., "natural language processing", "neural networks", "machine learning", or "robotics".
caption:
Share of artificial intelligence jobs among all job postings

A job posting is considered an AI job if it requests one or more AI skills, e.g., "natural language processing", "neural networks", "machine learning", or "robotics".

This line chart tracks the share of artificial intelligence jobs among all job postings from 2014 to 2024 across eight countries. The vertical axis ranges from 0% to 1.8%, while the horizontal axis marks years in two-year increments. The United States line, colored brown, begins near 0.6% in 2014 and rises steadily to reach 1.8% by 2024, remaining above all other countries throughout the period. The United Kingdom line, in orange, starts near 0.4% in 2014 and climbs to approximately 1.25% by 2024. Sweden’s purple line begins around 0.9% in 2019 and ends near 1.3% in 2024. Spain’s dark blue line starts near 1.05% in 2019, peaks around 1.35% in 2021, then declines slightly before rising again to about 1.25% in 2024. The Netherlands’ teal line begins near 0.6% in 2019, rises to 1.35% in 2022, then dips to 1.05% in 2023 before recovering to 1.25% in 2024. Austria’s green line starts near 0.6% in 2019, rises to 1.15% in 2022, then falls to 1.05% in 2023 and remains flat through 2024. Italy’s lavender line begins near 0.5% in 2019, rises to 0.7% in 2020, dips to 0.6% in 2022, then climbs to 0.85% in 2024. New Zealand’s light blue line stays below 0.6% for the entire period, starting near 0.15% in 2014 and ending near 0.55% in 2024. All lines show upward trends over time, with varying degrees of fluctuation between 2020 and 2024.
